D1.justification: Each claim's evidence basis is type-matched (AUC/sensitivity from ROC tables and figures for accuracy claims, DeLong p-values for comparison claims, Passing-Bablok/Bland-Altman for the method-comparison claim, CLSI precision runs for the analytical claim), all traceable to verbatim quoted lines.
D1.weakness: The 'excellent diagnostic accuracy' claims (C01, C04) are evidenced entirely by in-sample-derived cut-offs and AUCs with no held-out or externally validated comparator, so the evidence, while type-appropriate, cannot support accuracy generalizing beyond this cohort.

D2.justification: All 11 claims carry explicit, checkable falsification criteria tied to concrete statistical thresholds (CI overlap, DeLong p-value flips, slope/intercept CIs), which are independently re-computable from the filed tables.
D2.weakness: A few criteria are close to tautological restatements of the result rather than substantive risk (e.g., C06's 'recomputing grey-zone proportions and getting different values would refute it' just re-checks arithmetic, not a genuine empirical bet).

D3.justification: Claims are carefully hedged where the data warrant it (C08's decline trend explicitly flagged non-significant/exploratory, C05's 'adds little' framed cautiously, C01's interpretation note stating 'not external validation'), and problem.md's assumptions (A1-A5) make reference-standard and surrogate-outcome dependencies explicit.
D3.weakness: The confident sensitivity/specificity/AUC numbers in the claim statements themselves (C01, C04) are not hedged for the in-sample cut-off derivation noted only in constraints.md, creating a mismatch between the claim's assertive tone and the acknowledged optimism-bias caveat.

D4.justification: The observations (O1-O4) map cleanly to gaps (G1-G3) to a single key insight to nine experiments (E01-E09) to eleven claims to specific tables/figures, with dependency links (e.g., C04 depends on C01, E03 depends on E01/E02) consistent throughout and no contradictions across layers.
D4.weakness: The key insight bundles two distinct sub-claims (automated-platform parity and grey-zone/APOE utility) that are argued somewhat independently in the experiments below it, slightly diluting the single-insight structure.

D5.justification: The exploration tree records genuine node-level results and one honest negative (N13, renal-function avenue abandoned for a stated, defensible reason), which is good given this is a retrospective single-paper reconstruction.
D5.weakness: Only one dead-end is recorded and the two decision nodes (N02, N10) list alternatives with only single-sentence rejections, so deliberation depth is thin — largely inherent to reconstructing a published paper's post-hoc narrative rather than a live research trace.

D6.justification: The study reports appropriate baselines (cross-assay and cross-biomarker comparisons via DeLong), full CIs and p-values throughout, a stated power calculation, and CLSI-standard analytical validation, with limitations (no external validation cohort, in-sample cut-offs, underpowered renal subgroup) explicitly disclosed in constraints.md.
D6.weakness: No derivation/validation split was used for the diagnostic cut-offs (acknowledged as a limitation), and several supporting analyses (Supplemental Tables 1/5, Figure S1) are cited only from text since the underlying supplementary data were not available to the compiler.

findings.text: C01 and C04's 'excellent diagnostic accuracy' claims rest on Youden and two-cut-off thresholds derived and evaluated in the same 187-patient cohort with no derivation/validation split or external cohort; this optimism-bias risk is disclosed in constraints.md but not reflected in the claim statements' confident tone.

Exploration-tree deliberation is shallow (one dead-end, two decisions with one-line rejected alternatives), limiting how much genuine research judgment (vs. post-hoc narrative reconstruction of a single published paper) the trace actually captures.

Parts of the evidentiary basis for C10 and C11 depend on supplementary tables/figures (Supplemental Table 1, Table 5, Figure S1) that were not present in the provided PDF, so those sub-claims are grounded only in narrative text paraphrase rather than a verifiable table.
